overall_justification: The most prominent article, Dr. B. Philuer's commentary, strongly criticizes the financial program of the future president, specifically the "caixa" (Caixa de Conversão) and tariff proposals.  It links these proposals to currency devaluation and increased cost of living, using terms like "projectos sinistros" (sinister projects) and "desvalorizar a moeda nacional" (devalue the national currency), which align with Orthodox concerns. The article questions the motives of those supporting these proposals, suggesting they are acting against the nation's best interests.  While other articles mention the Caixa, none have the same weight or forceful argumentation as Philuer's piece.

supporting_evidence.quote: Não seria razoavel que nos mantivéssemos em duvida com relação ao programma financeiro do futuro presidente: S. Ex. quer a caixa do Sr. Campista, como quer as tarifas do Sr. João Luiz Alves — fataes apparelhos, que hão de trazer-nos, mesmo antes de funccionarem, os mais amargos soffrimentos.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This opening statement from Dr. Philuer's commentary sets the critical tone and clearly targets the "caixa" (Caixa de Conversão) as a source of future suffering.

supporting_evidence.quote: Ninguem poderá presumir que os dois esforçados representantes mineiros tomassem sobre os hombros, comquanto largos, a empreza de desvalorizar a moeda nacional e encarecer a vida do contribuinte, pugnando, no Congresso, pela passagem dos seus projectos sinistros...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote explicitly links the Caixa and tariff proposals to currency devaluation ("desvalorizar a moeda nacional") and increased cost of living, core tenets of the Orthodox argument.

supporting_evidence.quote: ...a caixa de conversão e o proteccionismo desatinado ensaiam alegremente seus cantos do victoria...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote uses emotionally charged language ("desatinado" - insane/reckless) to further condemn the Caixa and link it to potentially damaging protectionist policies, reinforcing the Orthodox stance.
